pi_items.source: Task scripts (setup_task.sh, export_result.sh, verifier.py) all use this consistent path
Used consistently in task.json description and verifier.py (line 76); conforms to synthetic test flight number pattern

pi_items.verified_value: AA100 is a real American Airlines flight operating from John F Kennedy International Airport (JFK) to London Heathrow (LHR), NOT from Dallas Fort Worth (DFW)
pi_items.source: Web search confirmed via Flightradar24, FlightAware, FlightStats, and other flight tracking sources. Multiple sources (airportia.com, planemapper.com, flightseatmap.com) confirm JFK-LHR route

pi_items.verified_value: CORRECT keywords for AA100: American (airline), London (destination), LHR (airport code), Heathrow (airport name), Scheduled/En Route/Landed (flight statuses). INCORRECT keywords: Dallas and DFW, which are NOT on the AA100 route (AA100 departs from JFK, not DFW). American Airlines does operate DFW-LHR flights on routes AA20, AA50, AA78, AA80, AA154, but NOT AA100.
pi_items.source: American Airlines flight routing verified via web search: AA100 is JFK-LHR; separate search confirmed AA does fly DFW-LHR but on different flight numbers (AA20, AA50, AA78, AA80, AA154, BA192)

Defined in task.json metadata but not actually used by verifier.py. The export_result.sh script captures report_size (line 20) but the verifier.py never checks this value against the minimum threshold. File size validation is absent from success criteria.

privileged_info_summary: American Airlines flight AA100 is a real commercial flight operating from John F Kennedy International Airport (New York) to London Heathrow, with Boeing 777-300ER aircraft, scheduled departure time 18:10 EDT and arrival 06:20 BST. The metadata incorrectly associates this flight with Dallas (DFW airport) keywords; however, AA100 does not service DFW-LHR route. Invalid flight number ZZ0000 is a synthetic test case. The task creates a report file at /sdcard/Documents/flight_search_report.txt. Note: the min_report_size_bytes metadata is unused in the verification logic.
